_meta.notes: Captured via in-repo TaskCompleted shim (pact-plugin/tests/runbooks/install_taskcompleted_logging_shim.sh) during in-session capture experiment on 2026-05-20. This is the LEAD-frame TaskCompleted stdin shape: 8 keys, neither teammate_name nor team_name present. The empirical absence of both discriminator fields IS the lead-context signature under the wake-lifecycle layer's compound field-presence check (`agent_id is None and teammate_name is None`). Sibling-shape relationship: this 8-key shape is the structural subset of the teammate-frame sibling (taskcompleted_teammate_context_shape.json) which equals these 8 keys PLUS `teammate_name` and `team_name`. A future reader comparing the two fixtures should see no other field-level differences. Sanitized: real session_id/transcript_path/cwd/task_subject/task_description replaced with example-placeholder values; payload shape preserved verbatim.

task_subject: example lead-driven TaskCompleted fire — captures the lead-frame stdin shape for fixture-parity testing
task_description: Example task description for a lead-driven TaskUpdate(status="completed") fire. The fire originates in the lead session (no teammate ownership); the empirical stdin shape carries neither teammate_name nor team_name. Used as the canonical lead-frame fixture for TaskCompleted hook tests.
